observations: Bundle master hash covers identity fields and three sub-hashes, but not winner_gene, engine, mutation counters, generation metadata, or strategies_tried (tools/evolution_bundle.py:503; tools/verify_bundle.py:329).
Verifier parses decimal fractions through binary float conversion and round-to-8-decimal scaling (tools/verify_bundle.py:99).
Bundle factory converts winner speedup through float division before Fraction reduction (tools/evolution_bundle.py:612).
LLM provider/model are resolved and returned, but EvolutionBundle serialization contains no provider/model provenance fields (lib/llm.ts:33; tools/evolution_bundle.py:379).
Production fitness uses a configurable 0.95 correctness gate while EBS verdict verification uses a fixed 0.90 gate (lib/fitness.ts:34; tools/verify_bundle.py:82).

hypotheses.statement: A validly hashed bundle can have decision-relevant provenance fields altered without invalidating verification.
hypotheses.basis: Hash coverage omits winner_gene and several serialized metadata fields.

hypotheses.statement: Malformed extreme numeric strings can cause verifier failure before fail-closed validation completes.
hypotheses.basis: _parse_fraction catches ValueError but not OverflowError from float conversion and integer rounding.

hypotheses.statement: Near-threshold speedups may classify differently across paths because one path uses float division before Fraction reduction.
hypotheses.basis: Winner speedup construction uses baseline_ms / winner_ms as a float before denominator limiting.

hypotheses.statement: A sealed bundle cannot independently establish which model/provider generated mutations.
hypotheses.basis: Model/provider exist in LLM results but are absent from EvolutionBundle.to_dict().

deductions: Integrity is partial rather than complete: hash_bundle authenticates the hashes and selected identity fields, while hash_fitness authenticates measurements/verdict and hash_genes authenticates gene records; omitted fields remain mutable under the verifier acceptance predicate.
Numeric threshold determinism is weaker than documented: exact Fraction verdict computation is undermined at the ingestion boundary by float-based decimal parsing and winner-ratio conversion.
The 0.95 demo gate can reject a gene in production evolution while a 0.90 EBS verifier gate can accept the corresponding declared verdict, creating policy drift.
Model provenance is not part of the evidence or integrity hash inputs, so a buyer cannot reconstruct or authenticate the mutation model from the bundle alone.
work_product.decision: Conditional fail for integrity_numeric scope: static review found material integrity coverage, numeric-boundary, and provenance gaps. No vulnerability is marked confirmed because execution and mutation were prohibited; these require isolated adversarial tests and remediation before claiming complete tamper evidence or numeric determinism.

adi.statement: The observed hash construction suggests omitted serialized fields could be changed while preserving all verifier recomputations.
If float conversion of an attacker-controlled numeric string raises OverflowError, verifier_bundle can terminate before returning a structured fail-closed result.
The codebase consistently guarantees exact threshold behavior across all bundle construction paths.
The serialized EBS record lacks authenticated provider/model provenance even though the LLM layer returns those values.
